title: AIDS 2026 Scholarship Programme
description: The International AIDS Society's scholarship programme funds in-person attendance at the 26th International AIDS Conference (AIDS 2026) in Rio de Janeiro, covering pre-paid economy return airfare, accommodation, a daily living allowance, and full conference registration for people working, volunteering, or studying in the HIV field. Priority goes to applicants from low- and middle-income countries, key populations, people living with HIV, and young professionals under 35. IAS runs this scholarship programme for every edition of the biennial International AIDS Conference.
visaNote: Brazil entry visa requirements vary by nationality; the scholarship covers travel insurance reimbursement (up to USD 80) but not visa fees.

title: Wikimania 2026 Travel Scholarship
description: The Wikimedia Foundation's Wikimania scholarship covers flights, accommodation, and registration for active Wikimedia community members to attend the annual Wikimania conference, held 21-25 July 2026 in Paris. Scholars are asked to contribute 4-6 hours of volunteer time during the event and are covered under the Foundation's travel insurance policy. Applications for the 2026 edition ran 10 September to 31 October 2025; the Foundation runs this scholarship for every annual edition of Wikimania.
visaNote: Scholars are responsible for obtaining any Schengen visa needed to enter France; not guaranteed by the Foundation.

title: Deep Learning Indaba 2026 — Financial Aid (Travel & Accommodation Grants)
description: The Deep Learning Indaba is the annual gathering of Africa's machine learning and AI research community, held in 2026 at Pan-Atlantic University in Lagos, Nigeria (2-7 August). Its financial aid route waives registration fees and, for a limited and competitive number of applicants, provides travel and accommodation grants; this route is open only to applicants living or studying in Africa. The 2026 financial aid application window closed 29 March 2026; the Indaba runs this same programme every year in a different African host country.
visaNote: Selected recipients travelling internationally within Africa may need a Nigerian entry visa; not guaranteed to be covered.

title: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America 2026 — Dan Kohn Scholarship
description: The Dan Kohn Scholarship Fund provides complimentary conference registration and, for recipients who need it, reimbursement of up to USD 2,500 in travel expenses (economy flights, train, or bus, plus hotel) to attend KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America, held 9-12 November 2026 in Salt Lake City. The program targets community members from underrepresented groups in cloud native and open source who could not otherwise attend. As of publication the dedicated scholarships page for the North America 2026 edition had not yet opened; CNCF runs this same program for every KubeCon + CloudNativeCon edition worldwide.
org: Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) / Linux Foundation
visaNote: US visitor visa or ESTA may be required depending on nationality; not covered by the scholarship.

title: Grace Hopper Celebration — AnitaB.org Advancing Inclusion Scholarship
description: AnitaB.org's Advancing Inclusion Scholarship Program funds travel, accommodation, and registration for students and professionals from underrepresented groups in computing to attend the Grace Hopper Celebration, held 27-30 October 2026 in Anaheim, California. Air tickets and hotel are booked directly by an AnitaB.org travel consultant, plus a travel subsidy intended to cover incidentals such as airport transport, baggage, and meals. The 2026 application window has closed; AnitaB.org runs this scholarship for every annual Grace Hopper Celebration.
visaNote: International applicants may need a US visitor visa; AnitaB.org does not guarantee visa assistance.

title: HPAIR Asia Conference 2026 — Delegate Scholarship
description: HPAIR's Asia Conference is an annual, student-run gathering on Asia-Pacific economic, political, and social affairs, hosted in 2026 at VinUniversity in Hanoi, Vietnam (19-23 August), drawing 300-600 delegates from 40+ countries. Delegates cover their own travel and lodging by default but can apply for a scholarship that reimburses some, or in select cases all, of these costs. The 2026 early-round scholarship deadline (31 March) has passed; HPAIR runs a similarly structured Asia Conference annually in a different regional host city.
org: Harvard Project for Asian and International Relations (HPAIR)
visaNote: Vietnam visa or e-visa is required for most non-ASEAN nationalities and is the delegate's own responsibility.

title: Global Youth Summit 2026 — Fully Funded Delegate Program
description: Global Changemakers' Global Youth Summit brings emerging community leaders aged 21-28 to Cape Town, South Africa (19-23 October 2026) for a fully funded leadership and social-impact program, with round-trip airfare, accommodation, and meals covered for selected participants. Applications required a two-minute video, written form, and a USD 35-45 processing fee; the 2026 round closed 20 April 2026. Global Changemakers, an independent youth NGO that grew out of a British Council initiative in 2007, runs an edition of this summit most years.
visaNote: South Africa visa fees, if applicable, are the participant's own responsibility and not covered by the funded package.

title: LACNIC 46 / LACNOG 2026 Fellowship Program
description: LACNIC's Fellowship Program provides financial support for members of the Latin American and Caribbean Internet community to attend its biannual meeting, held in 2026 as LACNIC 46 / LACNOG in Mendoza, Argentina from 19-22 October. Selected fellows receive a return economy-class ticket to the event and a waiver of registration fees, with accommodation covered for some categories of applicant. LACNIC does not cover visa fees, airport transfers, or airport taxes.
org: LACNIC (Latin American and Caribbean Internet Addresses Registry)
visaNote: Fellowship does not cover visa fees, airport transfers, or airport taxes; entry requirements depend on nationality.

title: APNIC Fellowship Program - APNIC 62 (Mumbai)
description: APNIC's Fellowship Program funds professionals, youth, and returning community members from emerging and developing economies in the Asia Pacific region to complete a project-based learning track and attend an APNIC conference, most recently APNIC 62 in Mumbai, India (3-10 September 2026). Fellows receive a return economy-class air ticket, single-occupancy hotel accommodation with breakfast, and a cash allowance of AUD 100-150 for incidentals. Applications for the 2026 cycle closed 13 March 2026; APNIC runs this fellowship roughly twice a year ahead of its two annual conferences, so a new call opens on a similar cycle.

title: Grace Hopper Celebration India (GHCI) Scholarship
description: AnitaB.org India's Advancing Inclusion Scholarship funds women and non-binary technologists to attend the Grace Hopper Celebration India (GHCI), India's largest gathering of women in computing. Recipients receive full conference registration, meals, accommodation, and a travel fare contribution of up to INR 3,000. The scholarship runs annually; at time of writing the current application window was closed, with a new one expected ahead of the next edition.

title: NeurIPS 2026 Financial Assistance Program
description: NeurIPS, one of the world's leading machine learning research conferences, runs an annual Financial Assistance program covering complimentary conference registration and a prepaid hotel stay for students, junior researchers, and other applicants for whom attending would otherwise be a financial barrier. NeurIPS 2026 takes place in Sydney, Australia, 6-12 December 2026. As of this research, the dedicated 2026 Financial Assistance application page had not yet been published; in past years it opened a few months ahead of the conference.
